introSection: Nostalgische Achterbahn is a steel roller coaster currently travelling with Norbert Witte.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride was manufactured by Schwarzkopf in 1967, debuting on the German fair circuit a year later under ownership of Purwin. The ride would be named Alpen Express during this time, continuing operation up until 1990, where it was sold to Hofmann & Jehn. They continued its operation on the German fairs up until 1998, after which they sold it to the Löwenthal family, who opened it in Skyline Park.

The ride opened in Skyline as Achterbahn, the same name the ride was given when it changed hands between Purwin and Hofmann & Jehn. It was originally situated close to the park entrance, but was relocated for the 2015 season, after which it changed its name to Nostalgische Achterbahn. It would eventually close again on 6 November 2022 and was sold to travelling showman Norbert Witte who intended to refurbish the coaster.

In 2025, the refurbishment project stalled as it was found major repairs would be required as the coaster had been damaged when it was dismantled.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Blue track and supports. The supports used to be unpainted.

fullText:
Nostalgische Achterbahn is a steel roller coaster currently travelling with Norbert Witte.

The ride was manufactured by Schwarzkopf in 1967, debuting on the German fair circuit a year later under ownership of Purwin. The ride would be named Alpen Express during this time, continuing operation up until 1990, where it was sold to Hofmann & Jehn. They continued its operation on the German fairs up until 1998, after which they sold it to the Löwenthal family, who opened it in Skyline Park.

The ride opened in Skyline as Achterbahn, the same name the ride was given when it changed hands between Purwin and Hofmann & Jehn. It was originally situated close to the park entrance, but was relocated for the 2015 season, after which it changed its name to Nostalgische Achterbahn. It would eventually close again on 6 November 2022 and was sold to travelling showman Norbert Witte who intended to refurbish the coaster.

In 2025, the refurbishment project stalled as it was found major repairs would be required as the coaster had been damaged when it was dismantled.



Blue track and supports. The supports used to be unpainted.
